like when the voltage is

LLM's don't only have
properties of utilities. I
think it's also fair to say
that they have some properties
of fabs. And the reason for
this is that the capex
required for building LLM is

just like building some power
station or something like
that, right? You're investing

think the tech tree and for
the technology is growing
quite rapidly. So we're in a
world where we have sort of
deep tech trees, research and

centralizing inside the LLM

analogy muddies a little bit
also because as I mentioned

is a bit less defensible
because it is so malleable. And so I think it's just an
interesting kind of thing to
think about potentially. There's many analogy analogies
you can make like a 4
nanometer process node maybe
is something like a cluster
with certain max flops. You

use when you're using Nvidia

some analogies here that make

the analogy that makes the
most sense perhaps is that in
my mind LLM have very strong
kind of analogies to operating

just electricity or water. It's not something that comes
out of the tap as a commodity.
this is these are now
increasingly complex software
ecosystems right so they're
not just like simple
commodities like electricity
and it's kind of interesting
to me that the ecosystem is
shaping in a very similar kind

have an open source
